design_philosophy.core_loop: Select play from playbook → execute formation with real-time input → resolve outcome → progress down field toward touchdown
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing offensive play selection and execution against opponent's defensive responses; territorial progression toward goal line
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control an NFL football team through a complete game, managing play-calling strategy and real-time player control to score touchdowns
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to large-scale on-screen action (all 11 players per side visible simultaneously) using isometric perspective; prioritizes visual spectacle and technical ambition for NES-era sports representation over simplified abstractions. Includes four-player modes with hidden play-card system for competitive fairness.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Single-player and multiplayer modes available; competitive depth emerges from play-selection strategy and real-time execution skill against variable AI or human opponents

progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight selectable NFL teams (based on 1989 season rosters with actual player stats, surnames, and numbers)
replayability_hooks: Multiple team rosters with different player stats and depth charts
Four-player competitive support via NES Four Score and NES Satellite adapters
Play-calling strategy variations across different team playbooks

world_building_techniques: Authenticity through 1989 NFL team cities and actual player rosters (original version only; Virtual Console uses generic names)
Post-game commentary by Nester (NES mascot character) announcing winner/loser

faction_indicators: Eight distinct NFL teams selectable with different rosters and play styles
Team selection serves as competitive identity in multiplayer modes
environmental_storytelling: Isometric field perspective and digitized voice callouts ('Touchdown!', 'First down!', 'Ready! Set! Hut!') ground the experience in authentic football broadcast language and visual conventions

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - NES Play Action Football (TCRF)
